en.extract:
The Fiji white-eye (Zosterops explorator) is a species of passerine bird in the white-eye family Zosteropidae. The species is also known as Layard's white-eye.

It is endemic to the islands of Viti Levu, Vanua Levu, Taveuni, Kadavu, and Ovalau in Fiji, where it is a common bird of forests. Where it co-occurs with the closely related silvereye it is more common in denser forest.
It is a typical small white-eye of the genus Zosterops, similar in appearance to the silvereye, although the plumage is much yellower, it is chunkier and has a complete eye-ring. The back is olive green and the throat and belly yellow. The call is described as "a high pitched seeu-seeu".
The Fiji white-eye feeds by gleaning insects from shrubs and trees. It will join mixed-species feeding flocks with other Fijian birds, including silvereyes. It also feeds lower down in the trees than silvereyes.

The '''Fiji white-eye''' ('''''Zosterops explorator''''') is a species of passerine [[bird]] in the white-eye family [[Zosteropidae]]. The species is also known as '''Layard's white-eye'''.<ref>Pacific Birds (2001) [http://www.pacificbirds.com/fiji1.htmlSummary List of the Birds of Fiji]. Downloaded  22 July 2008</ref>
[[file:Zosterops explorator Smit.jpg|thumb|left|Illustrated by [[Joseph Smit]] (1881)]]

It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the islands of [[Viti Levu]], [[Vanua Levu]], [[Taveuni]], [[Kadavu Island|Kadavu]], and [[Ovalau (Fiji)|Ovalau]] in [[Fiji]], where it is a common bird of forests.<ref name = "Pratt">Pratt, H., Bruner, P & Berrett, D. (1987) ''The Birds of Hawaii and the Tropical Pacific'' Princeton University Press:Princeton {{ISBN|0-691-08402-5}}</ref> Where it co-occurs with the closely related [[silvereye]] it is more common in denser forest.

It is a typical small white-eye of the genus ''Zosterops'', similar in appearance to the silvereye, although the [[plumage]] is much yellower, it is chunkier and has a complete eye-ring.<ref name = "Pratt"/> The back is olive green and the throat and belly yellow. The [[bird vocalization|call]] is described as "a high pitched ''seeu-seeu''".

The Fiji white-eye feeds by [[Gleaning (birds)|gleaning]] insects from shrubs and trees. It will join [[mixed-species feeding flock]]s with other Fijian birds, including silvereyes. It also [[Niche differentiation|feeds lower down]] in the trees than silvereyes.<ref>Langham (1989) "[http://www.notornis.org.nz/free_issues/Notornis_36-1989/Notornis_36_4.pdf Stratification of passerines in Fijian forests] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081017222920/http://www.notornis.org.nz/free_issues/Notornis_36-1989/Notornis_36_4.pdf |date=2008-10-17 }}". ''Notornis'' '''36''' (4): 267-285</ref>
